text: What if I told you AI doesn’t think, it predicts? Large language models, like transformers, work with tokens—think of them as puzzle pieces. They use attention mechanisms to focus on relevant pieces, predicting the next token based on context. But here's the catch: they can hallucinate, generating false information. So while they're powerful, they don’t 'understand'—they just predict. Want to learn more about AI? Hit that follow button and stay curious!
